title: Video of person in apparent KKK robe riles R.I. town
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News The hooded individual, who appeared to be wearing a white Ku Klux Klan robe, was seen walking around West Warwick.

By Abby Patkin April 29, 2026 | 2:58 PM 1 minute to read A Rhode Island community is on edge after social media videos appeared to show a person in a white Ku Klux Klan robe and hood walking around town in the middle of the night.
The West Warwick Police Department said it is investigating the incident, which happened on Main Street around 2 a.

title: ‘It’s doing exactly what art is supposed to do’: Readers react to MFA nude performance
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Readers Say Many defended Xandra Ibarra’s “Nude Laughing” as provocative, while others questioned whether it belonged at the MFA.

title: Jeremy Swayman named Vezina Trophy finalist for first time in his career
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins Jeremy Swayman ranked second among qualified NHL goalies this season with 28.
8 goals saved above expected.
Jeremy Swayman has bounced back from a lackluster 2024-25 season.

title: Local soccer fans puzzled by ‘unfortunate misstep’ after World Cup tailgating saga
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: World Cup The initial ban was met with dismay by local soccer supporter groups, who view tailgating as a vital part of the American sports culture.
Tailgaters show out early before a Patriots game at Gillette Stadium.

Jim Davis/Globe Staff After initially prohibiting tailgating for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium, the Boston 2026 World Cup host organization recently reversed the ban following online backlash and controversy.

title: Parent accused of assaulting bus driver in Canton
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The bus was transporting students to Hansen Elementary School, officials say.
The bus belonged to Canton Public Schools, officials said.
David L Ryan/The Boston Globe/FILE A parent allegedly assaulted a school bus driver in Canton Tuesday after a verbal exchange turned physical, officials said.

title: Red Sox ace Garrett Crochet lands on injured list with shoulder inflammation
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "It just makes more sense to get ahead of it now so I’m not playing catch-up the rest of the season.
" Garrett Crochet has a 6.
30 ERA through six starts this season.

title: Natick police officer on leave following domestic violence charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime “Things in this [case] are not what they seem to be,” an attorney for Jackson Dwyer said.

By Abby Patkin April 29, 2026 | 1:05 PM 2 minutes to read A Natick police officer is on paid leave after he allegedly suffocated his fiancée with a pillow during an argument at their home in Holliston Saturday, according to court records.
Jackson Dwyer, 28, pleaded not guilty Monday to charges of assault and battery on a family or household member, strangulation or suffocation, and witness intimidation.

title: 11 arrested outside Burlington ICE facility in act of ‘civil disobedience,’ police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The anti-ICE protesters refused to relocate to a "free speech area" that was identified by Burlington police.
An ICE car parked outside the agency's field office in Burlington in September 2025.

title: What are you reading at the beach for summer 2026?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Books Beach bags are not complete without a good book — or several.
What are you reading this summer?
Craig St Andre enjoys reading a Stephen King book seated on his chair with plenty of room at Wollaston Beach in Quincy.

title: Joel Embiid’s return is giving the 76ers a level of confidence that could make them dangerous
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "When he wants to be dominant, he will be," Tyrese Maxey said.
Joel Embiid dunks against the Celtics in the 76ers' Game 5 win.
Barry Chin/Globe Staff Joel Embiid took long, slow strides toward the podium and grimaced once he lowered himself into his seat.
